tickers.one_line_thesis: DataSection is betting the Neo Cloud franchise on hyperscaler-linked AI data centres in Japan (Inzai) and Australia, with a FY2026 revenue step-up to ¥33.6B still guided post-Apr-2026 delays; the May 2026 shift to NVIDIA B300 via Compal (~USD 325M) and a disclosed financing bridge improves execution visibility but raises contract-renegotiation, debt-timing, and Giga-order overhang risk.

deep_dive.executive_summary: DataSection is executing a step-change AI data-centre pivot (Neo Cloud / GPU capacity, TAIZA) with FY2026 guided revenue ¥33.6B vs FY2025 ¥2.9B (`3905.T/IR/en_financial_report/en-ir-20260416003.pdf`). 7 May 2026 disclosures sharpen execution: Inzai site, 5,080 B300 GPUs via Compal (~USD 325M), phased ops May–Jul 2026, and explicit financing bridge — but Giga B200 overhang (577 units, USD 252M unpaid) and contract amendments remain. Moat unproven, dhando none. At ¥4,490 (2026-05-25), base 5.7% scenario IRR on normalized ¥90/sh owner earnings. Marvin proposes watch.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Japan's dominant exchange and clearing infrastructure — a Stahl croupier with ~58% operating margins and volume leverage — but earnings are cyclical with market turnover; do not treat FY2025 peak activity as a permanent baseline.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Japan Exchange Group is a canonical Stahl croupier with stable moat and partial dhando. FY2025 (Mar-26) was exceptional — operating revenue ¥198.7B (+22.5%), margin ~58.5%, ROE 23.1% — driven by cash-equity turnover and clearing-related collateral income. Refresh at ¥2,000.5 (2026-05-25): base 4.7% 10yr IRR on ¥70/sh normalized owner earnings (below peak EPS ¥76.81). Marvin proposes watch at cycle-peak earnings — quality toll booth, not a 15%+ fat pitch without volume normalization or lower price.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Dominant e-commerce + AWS platform compounding at scale (FY2025 $717B sales, $78B NI) with AWS re-accelerating (+28% Q1-26) and high-margin ads/chips engines emerging — but AI capex has temporarily collapsed FCF, requiring disciplined valuation and capex ROI monitoring.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Amazon remains a multi-engine compounder (retail logistics, AWS, advertising, custom silicon) with stable moat and partial dhando. Q1 2026 accelerated ($181.5B sales +17%, AWS +28%), but AI capex has collapsed TTM FCF to $1.2B while OCF runs $148.5B. Decision-stack refresh at ~$263 (2026-05-25): base 3.6% 10yr IRR on normalized owner cash flow $5.35/sh (not spot TTM FCF). Marvin proposes watch — exceptional business, sub-15% return at price until capex normalizes or entry improves.

tickers.one_line_thesis: AI data centre developer with accelerating revenue ($126.6M FQ3, +139% YoY) and 600+ MW of signed leases (CoreWeave, hyperscaler), but GAAP losses, customer concentration, and a layered debt/preferred stack require capital-structure diligence before conviction sizing.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Applied Digital is a high-power AI data-centre developer/operator transitioning to lease-backed HPC hosting (600+ MW contracted). FQ3-26 (Feb-28-26) revenue $126.6M (+139% YoY) with $44.1M adj. EBITDA (ex-Cloud Services), but GAAP net loss to common $(100.9)M. Post-quarter $2.15B secured notes fund Polaris Forge 2. Moat unproven, dhando none. At ~$46.85 (2026-05-25), base 11.5% scenario IRR on forward $1.25/sh owner cash flow. Marvin proposes watch — operational inflection credible, capital structure and concentration keep gates closed.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global alternative-asset compounder earning durable fee streams on $614B fee-bearing capital with scaling insurance/wealth platform ($180B assets), active buybacks, and corporate simplification (BN+BNT merge) — DE-focused valuation, not GAAP NI.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Brookfield Corporation is a global alternative-asset platform with $614B fee-bearing capital (Q1-26), scaling insurance/wealth ($180B assets), and active simplification (BN+BNT merger path). Q1-26 distributable earnings (DE) $1.55B ($0.66/sh); LTM DE $2.54/sh. Stable moat, partial dhando. At ~$45.93 (2026-05-25), base 12.1% 10yr IRR on DE as owner earnings. Marvin proposes watch — quality compounder slightly below 15% hold band; realizations timing drives total DE.

tickers.one_line_thesis: CMSG is a cash-rich OTCQX bitcoin/scrypt miner where marked-to-market digital assets dominate GAAP earnings; the equity is largely a BTC-treasury and hash-share option under Horizon-affiliated governance — watch at 7.7% base IRR; fee redesign post-2026 and network-share trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Consensus Mining & Seigniorage (CMSG, OTCQX) is a Horizon-affiliated digital-asset miner whose economics split between thin operating mining (~$4.5M FY2025 revenue, ~$1.4M operating loss) and a large treasury (~$60.5M cash + ~$31.3M fair-value crypto at YE2025). GAAP earnings are dominated by ASU 2023-08 fair-value marks, not hash cash flow. At ~$30, base scenario IRR 7.7% fails the 15% bar; Marvin proposes watch (moat unproven, partial dhando). Upside is a BTC/treasury re-rating; primary risk is continued network-share loss and related-party fee drag post-waiver.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global salvage-vehicle auction toll booth de-rated to ~$33 — oligopoly economics intact; base IRR 9.2% (core override) while monitoring U.S. assignment/fee growth and Q3 FY2026 trends.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Copart remains a global salvage-auction oligopoly with fortress balance sheet and float economics, but the stock has de-rated to ~$33 (from a 52-week high near $61) while fundamentals still grow modestly. Q3 FY2026 (Apr 2026) delivered $1.24B revenue (+2.1% YoY) and $0.43 EPS with international strength offsetting −4% U.S. unit trends. At trough price, base 10yr IRR 9.2% still clears quality but not the 15% accumulate bar — Marvin proposes watch on math, while approved stance stays core (incumbent compounder override). Primary risk: prolonged U.S. assignment/fee stagnation, not balance-shee...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Wide-moat CRE platform de-rated to ~$33 (−54% YoY); Homes.com/Matterport distort GAAP — watch at 8.6% base IRR until normalized FCF proves out.

deep_dive.executive_summary: CoStar is the dominant U.S. CRE data and marketplace platform funding an expensive Homes.com and Matterport/Domain expansion. FY2025 revenue $3.25B (79% gross margin) with adj. EBITDA $480M, but GAAP net income $7M from amortization and marketing. The stock has fallen ~54% over 12 months to ~$33 — a de-rated wide-moat core with messy GAAP. Base 10yr IRR 8.6% on normalized FCF/sh fails the 15% bar → Marvin proposes watch. Primary risk: Homes.com burn without share gain.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Decentralized VMS acquirer reinvesting FCFA2S at hurdle rates — base 16.0% IRR supports core stance; GAAP net income secondary to FCFA2S compounding.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Constellation Software is the canonical serial acquirer of vertical market software (VMS) — deploying 100% of FCFA2S at hurdle rates through a decentralized BU model. FY2025 revenue $11.62B (+15%) with FCFA2S $1.68B (+14%); Q1 2026 accelerated ($3.18B rev, +20%; 6% organic). At ~$1,933 USD/sh (CSU.TO C$2,668), base 10yr IRR 16.0% clears the 15% hold band → Marvin proposes hold (math); approved stance core retained as incumbent allocator. Primary risk: M&A multiple inflation eroding reinvestment returns.

tickers.one_line_thesis: DBS-driven life science/diagnostics platform — hold at 10.1% base IRR (quality override); cyclically tied to biopharma funding and China diagnostics recovery.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Danaher is a DBS-driven life science and diagnostics platform (Biotechnology, Life Sciences, Diagnostics post-Veralto) with razor/blade consumables on installed bases. FY2025 revenue $24.6B, operating income $4.69B, FCF/sh ~$7.34. Q1 2026 revenue $5.95B, net income $1.03B — modest re-acceleration. At ~$172, base 10yr IRR 10.1% sits below the 15% hold band → Marvin proposes watch on math; approved stance hold retained (quality incumbent override). Primary risk: multi-year biopharma funding downturn + China diagnostics.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Horizon Kinetics–affiliated holding company with concentrated stakes in exchange infrastructure (MIH) and HKHC — a look-through bet on Murray Stahl's ecosystem, not an operating croupier; value = sum-of-parts vs fair-value accounting noise.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Search/YouTube ads cash engine plus Google Cloud AI infrastructure (+63% Q1 2026) — wide-moat compounder betting massive capex ($180–190B 2026) on Cloud ROI while navigating AI disruption to Search monetization.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Alphabet remains a dual-engine compounder: Google Services funds AI/cloud capex while Google Cloud +63% to $20.0B in Q1 2026. FY2025 revenues $402.8B (+15%); Q1 2026 $109.9B (+22%) with operating margin 36.1%. Moat widening on Cloud/data; Search faces long-run AI interface risk.

Decision-stack refresh at ~$386: Lawrence base 10yr IRR ~2.1% on reported FY2025 FCF/sh ($5.85) even with 11% growth — well below 15% hold band. Hohn medium-term EPS bridge (~18% 3yr on adj. EPS path) exceeds Lawrence near-term FCF math because 2026 capex guide $180–190B compresses cash yields. Stance proposal watc...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Global exchange and market-infrastructure croupier with 20 consecutive record revenue years and ~80% exchange margins — quality toll booth, but Lawrence base IRR ~11% at $156 sits below the 15% hold band on mid-cycle FCF; watch for pullback or bull-case volume.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Intercontinental Exchange is a canonical Stahl croupier: 20 consecutive record revenue years, exchange segment ~80% adjusted margins, minimal principal risk. FY2025 adj. FCF $4.19B; Q1 2026 net revenues $3.0B (+20% YoY) with energy +46% and financials +65% volume growth.

Decision-stack refresh at ~$153: Lawrence base 10yr IRR ~11.0% on mid-cycle FY2025 FCF/sh — below 15% hold band (unchanged thesis vs May 22 at $156). Hohn 3–5yr EPS bridge implies ~18% annualized on adj. EPS path × 22× exit — reconciles via cyclical FCF starting point vs forward earnings momentum. Stance proposal watch.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Michigan mineral royalty optionality (>1M acres, Copperwood/Highland Copper leases) with patient balance sheet — asymmetric upside if mining develops, steady burn and single-tenant concentration until royalties activate.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Keweenaw Land Association is a micro-cap Michigan mineral royalty optionality name: >1M net mineral acres, primary near-term economics from Highland Copper Copperwood leases (~$350K/yr FY2024), expanded by 667K-acre acquisition (~$1.52/acre, Jun 2024). Book value ~$12.70/share (Dec 2024); mgmt repurchased 51,633 shares @ $27 (Jun 2024).

Decision-stack refresh at ~$27: Lawrence base curve understates the case. Optionality overlay ([SSI KEWL](https://specialsituationinvesting.substack.com/p/keweenaw-land-association-kewl), [update](https://specialsituationinvesting.substack.com/p/update-kewe...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Mesabi Trust is a contractual iron‑ore pellet royalty on Cleveland-Cliffs’ Northshore (Silver Bay, MN): cash is cyclical commodity + bonus-tier optionality, with ongoing AAA arbitration (commenced Sep 2025) layered on statutory trust simplicity—watch until normalized run-rate distributions and dispute outcomes are clear.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Mesabi Trust is a passive iron-pellet royalty on Cleveland-Cliffs' Northshore Mining (Silver Bay, MN): 13,120,010 units; cash flows are tons shipped × realized pricing × bonus tiers — not Trust operating execution. Calendar Q1 2026 shipments 938,572 tons (+105% YoY) but bonus royalty $0 (first zero-bonus outcome in years except Covid idling); calendar Q4 2025 payment was $4.9M including $1.04M bonus.

Decision-stack refresh at ~$27: Q1 2026 bonus royalty $0 is a transitory gap (HK parallel to SJT suspension/reinstatement). Do not annualize depressed Q1 alone. Optionality overlay: normalized...

tickers.one_line_thesis: U.S. OTC market infrastructure and ATS operator — a Stahl croupier with subscription-heavy fees and expanding disclosure tiers (OTCID) and extended-hours trading — earnings scale with issuer count and activity, not balance-sheet risk.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
OTC Markets Group is a Stahl croupier: regulated U.S. OTC equity markets (OTCQX, OTCQB, OTCID, Pink) plus FINRA-member ATS infrastructure (OTC Link, extended hours). Revenue is subscription- and market-data-heavy with limited principal risk on core operations.

FY2025: Net revenues $107,882K; diluted EPS $2.58 (+14% YoY). Q1 2026: Net revenues $33,842K (+15% YoY); diluted EPS $0.59 (+18% vs prior-year quarter). ~11.86M diluted shares.

Decision-stack refresh at ~$52.11: base 9.7% 10yr IRR — below 15% hold band. Stance proposal: watch (quality niche croupier, not a fat pitch at ~22× run-rate...

tickers.one_line_thesis: Post-merger diagnostics platform (Quidel + Ortho) with improving adjusted margins but declining revenue — integration and non-respiratory growth must prove out before this is a compounder again.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
QuidelOrtho is a post-merger diagnostics turnaround (Quidel + Ortho 2022) — not a Stahl croupier. Labs, point-of-care, molecular, and donor screening. FY2025 revenue $2,730.2M (−1.9% YoY); adjusted EBITDA $597.0M (21.9% margin, +240 bps). Q1 2026 revenue $692.8M (−10.5% YoY per company deck). FY2026 guide: revenue $2.70–2.75B; adj. EBITDA $615–630M.

Decision-stack refresh at ~$12.18: base 10.9% scenario IRR on conservative owner cash (~$1.05/sh) — near but below 15% bar; dhando none, moat unproven. Stance: watch (gates fail for accumulate).

San Juan Basin Royalty Trust is a passive ~75% net-overriding royalty on Hilcorp-operated New Mexico gas properties — not a croupier; no moat. FY2025: $0 royalty income from Hilcorp; no distributions. Excess Production Costs $6,186,818 gross ($4,640,114 net to Trust) remain to recover before royalties resume. Cash reserves $14,380 at Mar 31, 2026; trustee on line of credit for expenses. May 18, 2026: no monthly distribution declared.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Essential financial intelligence — ratings, indices, and data subscriptions — a Stahl croupier / index infrastructure compounder with extraordinary margins; earnings leverage to issuance and AUM with cyclicality at the margin.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
S&P Global is a canonical Stahl croupier / index-infrastructure compounder: Ratings, Indices (AUM-linked), Market Intelligence, Platts, and Mobility (spin mid-2026). Fees on issuance, benchmarks, and data — minimal principal risk.

FY2025: Revenue $15.336B (+8%); GAAP diluted EPS $14.66; adjusted diluted EPS $17.83 (+14%). Q1 2026: Revenue $4.171B (+10%, +9% organic); adj. diluted EPS $4.97 (+14%); adj. operating margin 51.8%.

May 26, 2026 8-K: Market Intelligence President Saugata Saha departing Jul 30, 2026; 2026 guidance reiterated.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Swedish industrial serial acquirer of niche subsidiaries — decentralized Pabrai-style model with 2025–2026 margin/FCF turnaround underway; organic sales still negative while unprofitable units are pruned.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Teqnion AB is a Swedish industrial serial acquirer (listed First North 2019) owning 30+ decentralized niche-industrial subsidiaries. The 2025–2026 margin and FCF turnaround is visible — FY2025 EBITA +36%, Q1 2026 EBITA margin 14.3% (record) — while organic net sales remain −5% from deliberate pruning of unprofitable units. At ~158 SEK, base 13.7% 10yr IRR on FY2025 FCF/sh sits below the 15% hold band; Marvin proposes watch (quality compounder, not a fat pitch at this price). Thesis strengthening on operating proof; M&A discipline is the swing factor.

tickers.one_line_thesis: Delaware Basin produced-water infrastructure with strong Adj. EBITDA growth ($254M FY2025) and ~2,500 miles of pipe — asset-heavy midstream toll model, not Stahl croupier; thesis blocked by IPO/combination complexity and Five Point sponsor control.

deep_dive.executive_summary: WaterBridge Infrastructure operates produced-water gathering, disposal, and recycling in the Delaware Basin (~2,500 miles of pipe) under long-term fee contracts with E&P operators. Post-IPO (Sep 2025) combination scale drives strong growth: FY2025 revenue $525.6M (+66%), Adj. EBITDA $254.0M (+68%); Q1 2026 revenue $201M (+105%), Adj. EBITDA $102.9M (+105%), produced-water volumes 2,460 MBbl/d (+105%). Not a Stahl croupier — asset-heavy merchant infrastructure with volume cyclicality, $1.47B debt, and Five Point sponsor control.
